title: Enckea unguiculata
intro: Enckea unguiculata, also known as Encke's root, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands, belonging to the family Monimiaceae. The health benefits of Enckea unguiculata include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing pain and inflammation, as well as promoting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, such as encketine and other glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Encke's root is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, including tinctures, infusions, and decoctions, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including pain, fever, and respiratory issues.
